steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks why Miguel accompanies his brother. The answer must be grounded only in what the passage directly states — no inferences beyond the text are allowed.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Miguel has learned all he can from his brother...': it is the narrator who has learned all Alejandro could teach her, not Miguel. Miguel's motive is unrelated. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Miguel...wants to ask the sisters' famous father to be his mentor': the passage says Miguel wanted to "make the acquaintance" of the poet — a social introduction, not a request for mentorship. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Miguel aspires to present his poems to the sisters...': nowhere does the passage mention Miguel planning to share his own writing. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Miguel anticipates having the opportunity to be introduced to both the sisters and their father': the passage states Miguel was "hoping not only to meet us but to make the acquaintance of the poet himself" — exactly two goals, both captured here. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
